game_info.title: FLOTTENMANÖVER - Naval Combat
game_info.concept: **FLOTTENMANÖVER (Battleship):** FLOTTENMANÖVER offers two distinct modes of naval combat on a grid-based battlefield, controlled by tapping grid squares to select targets and confirming with a "Fire" button. In Classic Mode, two players secretly place 5 ships of varying lengths (e.g., a 5-square Carrier, a 4-square Battleship) on a 10x10 grid, then take turns calling out coordinates, with the first player to sink all opponent's ships winning. Commander Mode introduces unique naval commanders, each possessing 3 distinct special abilities (e.g., "Salvo" to fire 3 shots in a line, "Sonar Ping" to reveal ships in a 3x3 area) that consume 1-3 resource points per use, with 1 resource point generated each turn. This mode may feature larger grids (e.g., 12x12) and new L-shaped or T-shaped ship configurations, requiring players to strategically deploy abilities to gain an advantage. Progression involves completing daily missions like "Sink 5 ships with Commander X" to earn medals, increase player rank, and unlock new commanders or cosmetic ship skins.
game_info.description: FLOTTENMANÖVER is a tactical naval combat game where you command a fleet and strategically attack enemy ships on a grid battlefield. Your objective is to sink all 5 enemy ships before they sink yours. Ships are hidden until hit, requiring careful deduction and strategy. Use special commander abilities to gain tactical advantages like sonar scanning or multi-shot salvos. Earn resource points each turn to unleash devastating abilities. Victory requires both tactical thinking and resource management.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate grid cursor during targeting phase
SPACE: Confirm target selection and fire
W/A/S/D: Alternative grid navigation
Z: Cycle through available commander abilities
Shift: Activate selected ability (if enough resources)
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to title screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ship placement, targeting mechanics, and turn progression. Verifies that the AI can place ships, player can target cells, shots register hits/misses correctly, and turns alternate properly. Also tests that the grid updates visually with hit/miss markers.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically place ships randomly for both player and AI. Execute a series of strategic shots targeting different grid positions. Mix hits and misses to verify both outcomes work. Advance through multiple turns to ensure turn management works correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Ships should be placed without collision. Targeted cells should register as hits when ships occupy them and misses otherwise. Grid should update with appropriate visual markers. Turn counter should increment and switch between player/AI correctly. Test succeeds when 10+ shots are fired without errors.

Turn counter should increment and switch between player/AI correctly. Test succeeds when 10+ shots are fired without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by systematically sinking all enemy ships. Verifies that damage tracking works, ships sink when all segments are hit, and the game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state when victory is achieved. Also tests commander abilities activation and resource management.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use a systematic grid sweep pattern to hit all AI ships efficiently. Activate commander abilities when resources are available to test ability mechanics. Continue firing until all 5 enemy ships are completely destroyed. Monitor ship health and sink status throughout.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Each hit should damage ships correctly. When all segments of a ship are hit, it should register as sunk. When all 5 enemy ships are sunk, game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN. Commander abilities should consume resources and provide their intended effects. Test succeeds upon reaching victory screen.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition and AI targeting behavior. Verifies that AI can intelligently target player ships, track damage, and that the game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all player ships are destroyed. Tests defensive gameplay mechanics.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Allow AI to take multiple turns with minimal player offensive action. AI should eventually hit and sink player ships through random or intelligent targeting. Continue until AI destroys all player ships to trigger lose condition.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: AI should successfully target and hit player ships over multiple turns. Player ship health should decrease with hits. When all player ships are sunk, game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Lose screen should display correctly with restart option. Test succeeds when lose condition is reached.
TEST_3.description: Testing lose condition and AI targeting behavior. Verifies that AI can intelligently target player ships, track damage, and that the game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when all player ships are destroyed. Tests defensive gameplay mechanics.
TEST_3.strategy: Allow AI to take multiple turns with minimal player offensive action. AI should eventually hit and sink player ships through random or intelligent targeting. Continue until AI destroys all player ships to trigger lose condition.
TEST_3.expected: AI should successfully target and hit player ships over multiple turns. Player ship health should decrease with hits. When all player ships are sunk, game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Lose screen should display correctly with restart option. Test succeeds when lose condition is reached.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing special abilities system including Salvo (3-shot line attack), Sonar Ping (reveal 3x3 area), and Emergency Repair. Verifies resource generation, ability cost deduction, cooldowns, and that abilities provide correct tactical advantages without causing errors.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Resources should generate each turn (1 per turn). Each ability should consume correct amount (Salvo: 2, Sonar: 3, Repair: 2). Salvo should hit multiple targets in line. Sonar should reveal ships in area. Repair should restore health. Abilities should have proper cooldowns. Test succeeds when all abilities execute without errors.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause/unpause functionality and game state persistence. Verifies that ESC key properly pauses the game, all game state is preserved during pause, and gameplay resumes correctly when unpaused. Tests that paused overlay displays properly.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Start game and play several turns to establish game state. Press ESC to pause mid-game. Verify game freezes and pause overlay appears. Press ESC again to unpause and continue gameplay from exact same state. Repeat pause/unpause multiple times.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Pressing ESC during PLAYING phase should transition to PAUSED. All game elements should freeze (no turn progression, no animations). Pause overlay with "PAUSED" text should display. Pressing ESC in PAUSED should return to PLAYING with all state intact. Test succeeds after 3+ successful pause/unpause cycles.

Pressing ESC in PAUSED should return to PLAYING with all state intact. Test succeeds after 3+ successful pause/unpause cycles.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing restart functionality from game over states. Verifies that pressing R from either win or lose screen properly resets all game state, returns to START screen, and allows a fresh game to begin without any state contamination from previous session.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Complete a full game to reach GAME_OVER_WIN or GAME_OVER_LOSE. Press R to restart. Verify return to START screen with reset state. Press ENTER to begin new game. Verify ships are newly placed and all counters reset. Repeat for both win and lose scenarios.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Pressing R in game over state should reset gameState completely. Should return to START screen with title and instructions. All ships, scores, resources, and abilities should reset. Starting new game after restart should work identically to first game. Test succeeds after clean restart from both win and lose states.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing grid boundary conditions and ship placement validation. Verifies that ships cannot be placed out of bounds or overlapping, cursor navigation stays within grid boundaries, and edge cases like corner targeting work correctly without causing index errors.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Attempt to place ships at grid edges and corners. Navigate cursor to all boundary positions. Target corner and edge cells. Test that all boundary positions are accessible and functional. Verify no out-of-bounds errors occur during any operation.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Ship placement should reject out-of-bounds positions automatically. Cursor navigation should clamp to grid boundaries (0-9 for 10x10 grid). All edge and corner cells should be targetable and function correctly. No array index errors should occur. Test succeeds when all boundary cases handle gracefully without errors.
